question:
Why is the "bystander effect" considered a failure of social responsibility rather than simply rational self-interest?
A) Bystanders are legally prohibited from intervening in most public situations.
B) Because diffused responsibility leads each individual to assume someone else will act, resulting in collective inaction that harms the victim.
C) Intervening always makes the situation physically worse, so inaction is optimal.
D) The bystander effect only occurs when the bystanders personally know the victim.
